title: hypericum quadrangulum subsp. undulatum
intro: Hypericum quadrangulum subsp. undulatum, also known as the undulate St. John's wort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the St. John's wort family, characterized by its distinctive undulated leaves. The medicinal properties of Hypericum quadrangulum subsp. undulatum include its high content of hyperforin and hypericin, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant effects. This plant has been used for centuries to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, depression, and wounds, due to its health benefits, such as promoting wound healing and reducing inflammation. Hypericum quadrangulum subsp. undulatum can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
